checks.name: universality gate: chiral census S-sum = (127,127,127) = T(210)+2 T(126)+T(10) in every gauge factor
universality gate: 45 S-sum = (8,8,8) = T(45); full-theory b_SO(10) = 6 + 127 - 3*8 = 109 is i-independent
heavy-level list is complete: 21 unmixed + 2 R + 3 h + 5 t + chiral/eaten/vector mixed levels
DYN-1b ledger regression: triplet levels match heavy_spectrum.json
runner self-test: RK4 with two-loop off reproduces the one-loop analytic solution
textbook no-threshold MSSM unification: alpha1 = alpha2 near 2e16 GeV with alpha_G^-1 ~ 24-25 and a small alpha_3 mismatch
AG coefficient algebra: 10b1+6b2-16b3 = 120, 5b1+3b2-8b3 = 60, eps_ijk(b_i-b_j) weights = (4,-9.6,5.6), and 2/120 = 0.0167
benchmark x = 0.1 status DISCLOSED: exact alpha_1,2 matching either converges or is unphysical there (alpha_3 non-perturbative in the matching region)
fine x-scan: >= 6/10 points solved or explicitly disclosed as unphysical; the alpha_3 pull landscape over x is recorded
COMPATIBILITY POINT exists on the benchmark slice: some x gives |alpha_3 pull| < 3 sigma with M_S = 3 TeV (the unification window is non-empty)
SECONDARY 3x3 exact solve (x = 0.1, M_S free) converges; the M_S required for perfect unification is disclosed wherever it lands
Monte-Carlo propagation of input uncertainties succeeded on >= 100/120 samples at x*
compatibility WINDOW extracted at x* (16/50/84 percentiles), not a point claim
perturbativity DISCLOSED: above M_X the full-theory b = 109 puts the Landau pole a factor ~ exp(2 pi alpha_G^-1/109) above M_X (famous MSGUT feature, reported not hidden)
benchmark-neighborhood scan folded into the fine x-scan above (x = 0.20, 0.25 probe the accidental-zero region)

inputs_flagged.provenance: PDG-vintage benchmark values; DYN-4 target-table refresh will update

derivation_log.step_1_b_generator: every heavy level carries (S1,S2,S3) from AG Table 2 (re-derived in DYN-1b); universality gates: chiral sum (127,127,127), 45 sum (8,8,8), b_SO(10) = 6+127-24 = 109
derivation_log.step_2_running: RK4 on alpha_i^-1; SM 2-loop below M_S (b = (41/10,-19/6,-7)), MSSM 2-loop above (b = (33/5,1,-3), standard b_ij); gauge-only 2-loop, Yukawa terms neglected (flagged)
derivation_log.step_3_matching: supermultiplet-level one-loop matching at mu* = M_X = min(m_lambda_E, m_lambda_X)*m_scale (AG: lightest p-decay vector); Delta_i = (1/2pi) sum w*S_i*ln(M/M_X) with eaten pairs at m_lambda and vector superfields at weight -3*pair; scheme constants (2/21-type) dropped: DR-bar-style
derivation_log.step_4_ag_algebra: 10b1+6b2-16b3 = 120, 5b1+3b2-8b3 = 60, eps_ijk(b_i-b_j) = (4,-9.6,5.6), 2/120 = 0.0167: AG eqns (Deltasw, Deltath) coefficients machine-verified; S_W/S_X columns already gated in DYN-1b
derivation_log.step_5_solve: exact 3x3 Newton for (alpha_G^-1, ln m_scale, ln M_S) against alpha_1,2,3(M_Z); g in vector masses tied to alpha_G self-consistently; window from 200-sample MC over input errors (alpha_s dominant)

findings.kill_criterion_feed: this is a DYN-8 entry: the benchmark-slice exclusion is a reproducible, disclosed negative result
findings.pull_landscape_crosses_zero: between x = 0.12 and x = 0.14; compatibility point x* = 0.15 with pull +0.8 sigma
findings.unification_vs_proton_decay_tension: at x* the exact-matching solution forces M_X ~ 2e13 GeV and alpha_G^-1 ~ 52: d = 6 proton decay (tau ~ M_X^4) is then catastrophically fast -- the raw benchmark slice (lambda = eta = 1, benchmark gamma, M_S = 3 TeV) is excluded by unification + proton decay COMBINED; viability requires the (lambda, eta, gamma, complex xi) scan (AG's cancellation regions), deferred to a dedicated extension; quantitative tau_p = DYN-3
